labels: An enteric tube courses below the level of the diaphragm.
report:
=== VISUAL_FINDINGS ===
The right costophrenic angle is not fully included on the image. Enteric tube courses below the level of the diaphragm. A single frontal view of the chest was obtained. Large bore right-sided central venous catheter is seen terminating in the low SVC/cavoatrial junction. 

=== CLINICAL_EVIDENCE ===
Retrieved Medical Literature:

* FINDINGS: 
    * The right costophrenic angle is not imaged. Otherwise, the lungs are clear. The heart size is upper limits of normal. Enteric tube courses below the level of the diaphragm. There is no pneumothorax.
    * IMPRESSION: An enteric tube courses below the level of the diaphragm.
    * FINDINGS: An enteric tube tip is within the stomach. Heart size is normal. Mediastinal and hilar contours are within normal limits. Pulmonary vasculature is normal. Lungs are clear, however the left costophrenic angle is excluded from the field of view. No large right-sided pleural effusion or pneumothorax is detected on this supine exam. No acute osseous abnormality is detected.
    * IMPRESSION: Endotr
